detail.summary: A skill for designing task decomposition, role assignment, routing, loops, and result merging.

cardItems.title: orchestrator-designer
cardItems.description: A skill for designing collaborative multi-agent flows. It defines task decomposition, role assignment, routing, sequential and parallel stages, merge logic, and termination rules.

detail.orchestratorDescription: Items directly coordinated by the main orchestrator.

orchestratorItems.title: Task Decomposition and Role Assignment
orchestratorItems.description: Breaks a user request into work units and assigns the agent role needed for each stage.

orchestratorItems.title: Sequential, Parallel, and Routing Structure
orchestratorItems.description: Determines which stages run sequentially, which run in parallel, and when routing should switch to another agent.

orchestratorItems.title: Input Handoff and Merge Logic
orchestratorItems.description: Defines what output becomes the next input and how multiple agent results are compared or merged.

orchestratorItems.title: Loops and Termination Rules
orchestratorItems.description: Controls loop entry, retry rules, fallback paths, and termination conditions so the flow converges cleanly.

detail.triggerDescription: Request types that call for this skill.

triggerItems.title: Role-Split Requests
triggerItems.description: Triggers when two or more agents with different responsibilities are needed.

triggerItems.title: Collaborative Flow Design
triggerItems.description: Covers collaboration, chaining, pipelines, parallel execution, routing, and result integration.

detail.patternDescription: Structural patterns commonly handled by the orchestrator.

patternItems.title: Hierarchical Structure
patternItems.description: A structure with one main orchestrator coordinating specialist sub-agents.

patternItems.title: Sequential and Parallel Flow
patternItems.description: Includes sequential pipelines, parallel fan-out, and convergence stages where outputs merge again.

patternItems.title: Conditional Control
patternItems.description: Combines routing conditions, feedback loops, and human checkpoints where needed.

detail.principleDescription: Guidelines for keeping the structure stable and predictable.

principleItems.title: Explicit Context Handoff
principleItems.description: Passes file paths, previous outputs, and evaluation criteria explicitly between stages.

principleItems.title: Simple Hierarchy
principleItems.description: Keeps the main orchestrator responsible for the full flow instead of building deep nested layers.

principleItems.title: Role-Based Constraints
principleItems.description: Separates tool and model policies by role and caps iterative loops.

principleItems.title: Predefined Output Rules
principleItems.description: Defines output formats and termination rules in advance to reduce ambiguity between stages.

detail.outputDescription: Elements included in the final orchestrator document.

outputItems.title: Overview and Premise
outputItems.description: Defines the orchestrator overview and implementation premise first.

outputItems.title: Relationship, Branching, and Loop Diagram
outputItems.description: Visualizes agent relationships, branching, and loops with Mermaid diagrams.

outputItems.title: Role, I/O, and Handoff Document
outputItems.description: Documents role definitions, inputs and outputs, handoff, retry behavior, and fallback strategy.

outputItems.title: Operating Rules
outputItems.description: Specifies routing rules, parallel stages, merge logic, and termination conditions at the document level.

detail.summary: An MCP index connecting Unity and Blender work contexts.

harness.title: Harness
harness.description: A set of repeatable AI implementation and evaluation harnesses

detail.summary: Harness structures with role separation and test, evaluation, and review loops
detail.patternsDescription: A harness index grouped by implementation, validation, regression, and documentation sync purposes.

patternsItems.title: TheHabit Implementation Harness
patternsItems.description: Architect plans, Coder implements, failed tests trigger a Debugger + Coder fix loop, and Reviewer closes the flow.

loops.label: When tests fail, the flow returns from debug to implementation.

patternsItems.title: Dev Harness
patternsItems.description: A spec-driven implementation harness that loads a JSON spec, prepares a branch, runs Codex, tests the result, and can finish with commit and PR creation.

patternsItems.title: Eval Harness
patternsItems.description: A validation harness that runs 50+ scenarios through the live pipeline to verify routing, tool usage, and result status.

patternsItems.title: Improve Harness
patternsItems.description: A self-improvement loop where a User agent generates requests, an Evaluator grades outcomes, and a Developer fixes failed items before regression checks.

loops.label: If regression checks still fail, the flow goes back into execution and evaluation.

patternsItems.title: Doc Harness
patternsItems.description: A documentation sync harness that analyzes code-doc drift, updates docs, then verifies and re-verifies the result.

loops.label: If mismatches remain after verification, the flow returns to updating docs.

detail.abstractDescription: Architecture principles shared across the harness system.

abstractItems.title: Role separation
abstractItems.description: Planning, implementation, evaluation, and review are split instead of being overloaded onto one agent.

abstractItems.title: Built-in quality gates
abstractItems.description: Testing, review, or scenario evaluation always follows execution as an explicit gate.

abstractItems.title: Feedback loops
abstractItems.description: Failure does not terminate the flow immediately; feedback is routed back into the implementation stage.

abstractItems.title: Repeatability
abstractItems.description: Artifacts such as tasks.yaml, spec JSON, logs, and harness-results make the loops reusable across runs.
